public_reputation: Known around Cosmoria through day-to-day work as porter.
manner: Judges the world by weight, delay, and whether moving one thing will jam five others behind it. In Cosmoria, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of porter in and around Cosmoria, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Cosmoria workable through the obligations of porter while managing the immediate pressure of storm weather, trade vulnerability.
hidden_pressure: Must keep the role of porter credible while absorbing the strain created by storm weather, trade vulnerability.

adventure_hooks.summary: Aurek Tideglass can draw the party into a locality problem centered on porter duties in Cosmoria.
Aurek Tideglass becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with storm weather, trade vulnerability around Cosmoria.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.
public_signal: In public, Aurek Tideglass is immediately readable as a porter tied to Cosmoria.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Cosmoria: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Aurek Tideglass presents as someone shaped by the duties of a porter and the daily pressures of Cosmoria.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a porter, and proof that strangers will not make Cosmoria harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Aurek Tideglass refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a porter too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can move people or goods quickly and knows which cargo draws the wrong attention.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Aurek Tideglass adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Aurek Tideglass always knows more about Cosmoria than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a porter in Cosmoria.
